prompt:
On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, the file-integrity monitoring tool (AIDE) must have an established baseline so it can later detect unauthorized modifications to system files; without an initialized known-good database, integrity checks cannot function. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that ensures the file-integrity tool is present and that a valid baseline integrity database has been generated and installed in its active location so that subsequent integrity checks succeed.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

AIDE_BIN="/usr/bin/aide"
AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide/aide.conf"
AIDE_DB_DIR="/var/lib/aide"
AIDE_DB_NEW="${AIDE_DB_DIR}/aide.db.new"
AIDE_DB_GZ_NEW="${AIDE_DB_NEW}.gz"
AIDE_DB_ACTIVE="${AIDE_DB_DIR}/aide.db"
AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE="${AIDE_DB_ACTIVE}.gz"

need_root() {
  if [[ "${EUID}" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
}

install_aide() {
  if ! command -v aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    apt-get update -y
    apt-get install -y aide aide-common
  fi
}

ensure_config() {
  if [[ ! -f "${AIDE_CONF}" ]]; then
    if [[ -f /etc/aide/aide.conf.dpkg-dist ]]; then
      cp -f /etc/aide/aide.conf.dpkg-dist "${AIDE_CONF}"
    elif [[ -f /usr/share/aide/config/aide.conf ]]; then
      cp -f /usr/share/aide/config/aide.conf "${AIDE_CONF}"
    fi
  fi

  if [[ -f "${AIDE_CONF}" ]]; then
    if ! grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*database_new[[:space:]]*=' "${AIDE_CONF}"; then
      printf '\ndatabase_new=file:%s\n' "${AIDE_DB_NEW}" >> "${AIDE_CONF}"
    fi
    if ! grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*database[[:space:]]*=' "${AIDE_CONF}"; then
      printf 'database=file:%s\n' "${AIDE_DB_ACTIVE}" >> "${AIDE_CONF}"
    fi
  fi
}

generate_baseline() {
  mkdir -p "${AIDE_DB_DIR}"

  if [[ -x "${AIDE_BIN}" ]]; then
    if [[ ! -s "${AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE}" ]]; then
      if aideinit >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        :
      else
        if [[ -f "${AIDE_DB_GZ_NEW}" ]]; then
          cp -f "${AIDE_DB_GZ_NEW}" "${AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE}"
        elif [[ -f "${AIDE_DB_NEW}" ]]; then
          gzip -c "${AIDE_DB_NEW}" > "${AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE}"
        else
          if [[ -f "${AIDE_CONF}" ]]; then
            aide --init --config="${AIDE_CONF}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
          fi
        fi
      fi
    fi
  fi

  if [[ -s "${AIDE_DB_GZ_NEW}" && ! -s "${AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE}" ]]; then
    cp -f "${AIDE_DB_GZ_NEW}" "${AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE}"
  fi

  if [[ -s "${AIDE_DB_NEW}" && ! -s "${AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE}" ]]; then
    gzip -c "${AIDE_DB_NEW}" > "${AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE}"
  fi

  if [[ -s "${AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE}" && ! -s "${AIDE_DB_ACTIVE}" ]]; then
    gunzip -c "${AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE}" > "${AIDE_DB_ACTIVE}"
  fi

  if [[ ! -s "${AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE}" && -s "${AIDE_DB_ACTIVE}" ]]; then
    gzip -c "${AIDE_DB_ACTIVE}" > "${AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE}"
  fi
}

verify_baseline() {
  if [[ -s "${AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE}" ]]; then
    return 0
  fi
  if [[ -s "${AIDE_DB_ACTIVE}" ]]; then
    gzip -c "${AIDE_DB_ACTIVE}" > "${AIDE_DB_GZ_ACTIVE}"
    return 0
  fi
  return 1
}

need_root
install_aide
ensure_config
generate_baseline
verify_baseline

exit 0
```

prompt:
On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, configure the AIDE file-integrity tool so that it monitors and verifies the integrity of the system's audit tools (the binaries used to view, manipulate, and report on audit records). Each of these audit tool executables must be registered with AIDE for cryptographic verification covering their permissions, ownership, and content hashes, so that any modification, replacement, or tampering of the tools can be detected. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that ensures the audit tools are tracked by the file-integrity tool, with the configuration persisting across reboots.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

AIDE_CONF_DIR="/etc/aide"
AIDE_CONF_FILE="${AIDE_CONF_DIR}/aide.conf"
AIDE_EXTRA_FILE="${AIDE_CONF_DIR}/aide.conf.d/99-audit-tools.conf"

AUDIT_TOOLS=(
  /usr/sbin/auditctl
  /usr/sbin/aureport
  /usr/sbin/ausearch
  /usr/sbin/autrace
  /usr/sbin/audispd
  /usr/sbin/auditd
  /usr/sbin/augenrules
)

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  apt-get update -y
  DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y aide
fi

mkdir -p "${AIDE_CONF_DIR}/aide.conf.d"

cat > "${AIDE_EXTRA_FILE}" <<'EOF'
# Managed by remediation script: track audit tools for integrity verification
EOF

for tool in "${AUDIT_TOOLS[@]}"; do
  if [[ -e "$tool" ]]; then
    printf '%s p+i+n+u+g+s+m+c+sha256\n' "$tool" >> "${AIDE_EXTRA_FILE}"
  fi
done

if [[ ! -f "${AIDE_CONF_FILE}" ]]; then
  touch "${AIDE_CONF_FILE}"
fi

if ! grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*@@include[[:space:]]+/etc/aide/aide\.conf\.d/\*\.conf[[:space:]]*$' "${AIDE_CONF_FILE}"; then
  if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*@@include[[:space:]]+/etc/aide/aide\.conf\.d/[[:alnum:]_.-]+[[:space:]]*$' "${AIDE_CONF_FILE}"; then
    :
  else
    printf '\n@@include /etc/aide/aide.conf.d/*.conf\n' >> "${AIDE_CONF_FILE}"
  fi
fi

if [[ -f /etc/default/aide ]]; then
  sed -i 's/^[[:space:]]*CRON_DAILY_RUN=.*/CRON_DAILY_RUN=yes/' /etc/default/aide || true
  grep -q '^[[:space:]]*CRON_DAILY_RUN=' /etc/default/aide || echo 'CRON_DAILY_RUN=yes' >> /etc/default/aide
fi

if [[ -f /etc/cron.daily/aide ]]; then
  chmod 0755 /etc/cron.daily/aide
fi

if [[ -f /etc/systemd/system/aidecheck.service ]]; then
  systemctl daemon-reload || true
fi

exit 0
```

prompt:
On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, the AIDE file-integrity tool is used to detect unauthorized changes to baseline system files, but undetected changes are only useful if responsible staff are alerted. Configure the system so that whenever the periodic AIDE integrity scan completes, the results of that scan are automatically delivered by email to the local root mailbox, ensuring administrators are notified of any detected modifications. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that ensures the scheduled AIDE check reports its scan details via email and that this configuration persists across reboots.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

AIDE_CONF="/etc/default/aide"
AIDE_CRON="/etc/cron.daily/aide"
MAIL_CMD="/usr/bin/mail"
MAIL_PKG="mailutils"

if ! command -v aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  exit 0
fi

if ! command -v mail >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  apt-get update -y >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  apt-get install -y "$MAIL_PKG" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if [ -f "$AIDE_CONF" ]; then
  if grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*MAILTO=' "$AIDE_CONF"; then
    sed -i 's|^[[:space:]]*MAILTO=.*|MAILTO=root|' "$AIDE_CONF"
  else
    printf '\nMAILTO=root\n' >> "$AIDE_CONF"
  fi
else
  printf 'MAILTO=root\n' > "$AIDE_CONF"
fi

if [ -f "$AIDE_CRON" ]; then
  if grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*MAILTO=' "$AIDE_CRON"; then
    sed -i 's|^[[:space:]]*MAILTO=.*|MAILTO=root|' "$AIDE_CRON"
  else
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    {
      printf 'MAILTO=root\n'
      cat "$AIDE_CRON"
    } > "$tmp"
    cat "$tmp" > "$AIDE_CRON"
    rm -f "$tmp"
  fi
fi

if [ -x "$AIDE_CRON" ]; then
  if ! grep -qE 'mail\s+-s\s+.*root' "$AIDE_CRON"; then
    cp -a "$AIDE_CRON" "${AIDE_CRON}.bak.$(date +%s)" 2>/dev/null || true
    perl -0pi -e '
      s{
        (^.*aide.*--check.*\n?)
      }{
        my $line = $1;
        $line =~ s/\n$//;
        $line . " | " . q{/usr/bin/mail -s "AIDE integrity scan results" root} . "\n";
      }mge
    ' "$AIDE_CRON" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
fi

if [ -x "$AIDE_CRON" ] && ! grep -qE 'mail\s+-s\s+.*root' "$AIDE_CRON"; then
  cat > "$AIDE_CRON" <<'EOF'
#!/bin/sh
set -e
if [ -x /usr/bin/aide ] && [ -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz ]; then
  /usr/bin/aide --check 2>&1 | /usr/bin/mail -s "AIDE integrity scan results" root
fi
EOF
  chmod 0755 "$AIDE_CRON"
fi

if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  systemctl daemon-reload >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0
```

prompt:
On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, the AIDE file-integrity tool monitors critical files for unauthorized changes, but Access Control List (ACL) attributes can grant permissions beyond the standard file mode and must also be tracked. Configure AIDE so that every file-monitoring ruleset verifies ACLs, ensuring that any ACL change on monitored files is detected during integrity checks. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that ensures all AIDE rulesets include ACL verification.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.

On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, configure the AIDE file-integrity tool so that every defined check ruleset also verifies files' extended attributes, since extended attributes can carry security-relevant metadata that would otherwise go unmonitored for tampering. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that ensures extended-attribute checking is enabled across all rulesets in the AIDE configuration, in a way that persists.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

CONFIG_FILE="/etc/aide/aide.conf"

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]]; then
  echo "AIDE configuration file not found: $CONFIG_FILE" >&2
  exit 1
fi

tmp="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT

awk '
function trim(s) {
  sub(/^[[:space:]]+/, "", s)
  sub(/[[:space:]]+$/, "", s)
  return s
}
function has_xattr(rule,   n,a,i,t) {
  n = split(rule, a, /[[:space:]]+/)
  for (i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
    t = a[i]
    if (t == "xattrs" || t == "X") return 1
  }
  return 0
}
function add_xattr(rule,   n,a,i,out,t) {
  n = split(rule, a, /[[:space:]]+/)
  out = ""
  for (i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
    t = a[i]
    if (t == "") continue
    if (out == "") out = t
    else out = out " " t
  }
  if (out == "") return "xattrs"
  return out " xattrs"
}
{
  line = $0
  stripped = line
  sub(/[[:space:]]*#.*$/, "", stripped)
  if (stripped ~ /^[[:space:]]*$/) {
    print line
    next
  }

  if (stripped ~ /^[[:space:]]*=/) {
    print line
    next
  }

  if (stripped ~ /^[[:space:]]*[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+[[:space:]]*=/) {
    match(stripped, /^[[:space:]]*([A-Za-z0-9_.-]+)[[:space:]]*=/, m)
    name = m[1]
    if (name == "xattrs") {
      print line
      next
    }
    if (has_xattr(stripped)) {
      print line
      next
    }

    pos = index(line, "=")
    lhs = substr(line, 1, pos)
    rhs = substr(line, pos + 1)

    comment = ""
    if (match(rhs, /[[:space:]]*#.*$/)) {
      comment = substr(rhs, RSTART)
      rhs = substr(rhs, 1, RSTART - 1)
    }

    rhs_trim = trim(rhs)
    if (rhs_trim == "") {
      print line
      next
    }

    newrhs = add_xattr(rhs_trim)
    print lhs newrhs comment
    next
  }

  print line
}
' "$CONFIG_FILE" > "$tmp"

if ! cmp -s "$CONFIG_FILE" "$tmp"; then
  cp --preserve=mode,ownership,timestamps "$tmp" "$CONFIG_FILE"
fi

if command -v aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if [[ -x /usr/sbin/aideinit ]]; then
    :
  fi
fi
```

    printf 'GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="%s"\n' "$FIPS_GRUB_PARAM" >>"$file"
  fi

  for key in GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT; do
    local line val
    line="$(grep -E "^[[:space:]]*${key}=" "$file" | tail -n1 || true)"
    [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue

    for token in $FIPS_KERNEL_PARAM; do

  done
}

    local out="/boot/grub/grub.cfg"

EOF

  if have_cmd sed; then
    sed -i -E 's/[[:space:]]+/ /g' "$GRUB_D_FILE"
  fi

}

enable_fips_crypto_policy() {

    cat >"$FIPS_MODULES_FILE" <<'EOF'

hash = -SHA1

group = -X25519 -X448
EOF
    printf '%s\n' "$MODE" >"$FIPS_POLICY_FILE"

ensure_fips_boot_marker() {
  touch "$FIPS_BOOT_MARKER"
}

ensure_fips_packages() {
  if have_cmd apt-get; then
    export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
    apt-get update -y
    apt-get install -y ubuntu-fips fips-initramfs || true
  fi
}

ensure_fips_kernel_installed() {
  if have_cmd apt-cache && have_cmd apt-get; then
    if ! dpkg -l | awk '{print $2}' | grep -qx 'linux-image-fips'; then
      apt-get install -y linux-image-fips || true
    fi
  fi
}

ensure_fips_mode_now() {
  ensure_fips_boot_marker
  enable_fips_crypto_policy
  ensure_fips_packages
  ensure_fips_kernel_installed
  enable_fips_kernel_boot
  if have_cmd update-initramfs; then
    update-initramfs -u -k all
  fi
}

verify_or_warn() {
  local ok=1
  if [[ -f "$FIPS_BOOT_MARKER" ]]; then :; else ok=0; fi
  if grep -Rqs -- 'fips=1' /etc/default/grub /etc/default/grub.d 2>/dev/null; then :; else ok=0; fi

  if [[ $ok -ne 1 ]]; then
    log "FIPS remediation applied; reboot required to activate kernel FIPS mode."
  fi
}

ensure_fips_mode_now
verify_or_warn
exit 0

Write an idempotent bash remediation script that establishes this system-wide cryptographic policy and ensures it persists across reboots.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

POLICY="DEFAULT"

if [[ "${EUID}" -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v update-crypto-policies >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "update-crypto-policies is not installed on this system." >&2
  exit 1
fi

current_policy="$(update-crypto-policies --show 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [[ "${current_policy}" != "${POLICY}" ]]; then
  update-crypto-policies --set "${POLICY}"
fi

if [[ -f /etc/crypto-policies/config ]]; then
  if [[ "$(tr -d '[:space:]' < /etc/crypto-policies/config)" != "${POLICY}" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${POLICY}" > /etc/crypto-policies/config
  fi
else
  install -d -m 0755 /etc/crypto-policies
  printf '%s\n' "${POLICY}" > /etc/crypto-policies/config
fi

if [[ -d /etc/crypto-policies/back-ends ]]; then
  if command -v update-crypto-policies >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    update-crypto-policies --set "${POLICY}" >/dev/null
  fi
fi

if command -v update-ca-certificates >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  update-ca-certificates >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

The relevant crypto-policy back-end for the SSH server must contain the correct cipher specification, uncommented and aligned with the system policy. response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

BACKEND_FILE="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver.config"
POLICY_FILE="/etc/crypto-policies/policies/FIPS.pol"
POLICY_LINK="/etc/crypto-policies/config"
DESIRED_CIPHERS="aes256-gcm@openssh.com,aes128-gcm@openssh.com,aes256-ctr,aes192-ctr,aes128-ctr"

mkdir -p /etc/crypto-policies/back-ends /etc/crypto-policies/policies

if [[ ! -f "$POLICY_FILE" ]]; then
  cat > "$POLICY_FILE" <<'EOF'
# FIPS system-wide cryptographic policy
cipher = aes256-gcm@openssh.com,aes128-gcm@openssh.com,aes256-ctr,aes192-ctr,aes128-ctr
EOF
fi

ln -sfn "$POLICY_FILE" "$POLICY_LINK"

if [[ ! -f "$BACKEND_FILE" ]]; then
  touch "$BACKEND_FILE"
fi

tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$tmpfile"' EXIT

awk -v desired="$DESIRED_CIPHERS" '
BEGIN { found=0 }
{
  if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*cipher[[:space:]]*=/) {
    print "cipher = " desired
    found=1
  } else if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*cipher[[:space:]]*=/) {
    print "cipher = " desired
    found=1
  } else {
    print $0
  }
}
END {
  if (!found) print "cipher = " desired
}
' "$BACKEND_FILE" > "$tmpfile"

install -m 0644 "$tmpfile" "$BACKEND_FILE"

if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  systemctl try-reload-or-restart ssh.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  systemctl try-reload-or-restart sshd.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

prompt:
On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, the OpenSSH server's message authentication code (MAC) algorithms must be governed by the system-wide cryptographic policy rather than by a local override, ensuring only FIPS 140-2 validated MACs are used and that crypto configuration remains centralized and consistent. A local override that diverges from the system crypto policy weakens assurance and fragments configuration. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that ensures the SSH server's MAC algorithm selection conforms to the system-wide cryptographic policy back-end, persisting across reboots.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_D="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
BACKUP_DIR="/var/backups"
STAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
BACKUP_FILE="${BACKUP_DIR}/sshd_config.${STAMP}.bak"

mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

backup_file() {
  local f="$1"
  if [[ -f "$f" ]]; then
    cp -a -- "$f" "$BACKUP_FILE"
  fi
}

remove_mac_directives() {
  local f="$1"
  [[ -f "$f" ]] || return 0
  local tmp
  tmp="$(mktemp)"
  awk '
    BEGIN { IGNORECASE=1 }
    /^[[:space:]]*MACs[[:space:]]+/ { next }
    /^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*MACs[[:space:]]+/ { next }
    { print }
  ' "$f" > "$tmp"
  if ! cmp -s "$f" "$tmp"; then
    backup_file "$f"
    cat "$tmp" > "$f"
  fi
  rm -f "$tmp"
}

remove_mac_from_dropins() {
  [[ -d "$SSHD_CONFIG_D" ]] || return 0
  local f tmp changed
  shopt -s nullglob
  for f in "$SSHD_CONFIG_D"/*.conf; do
    [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    awk '
      BEGIN { IGNORECASE=1 }
      /^[[:space:]]*MACs[[:space:]]+/ { next }
      /^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*MACs[[:space:]]+/ { next }
      { print }
    ' "$f" > "$tmp"
    if ! cmp -s "$f" "$tmp"; then
      backup_file "$f"
      cat "$tmp" > "$f"
    fi
    rm -f "$tmp"
  done
  shopt -u nullglob
}

ensure_crypto_policy_backend() {
  local policy_file="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver.config"
  local include_line="Include /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf"
  local dropin_file="${SSHD_CONFIG_D}/00-crypto-policy.conf"

  mkdir -p "$SSHD_CONFIG_D"

  if [[ -f "$policy_file" ]]; then
    if [[ ! -f "$dropin_file" ]] || ! cmp -s "$dropin_file" <(printf '%s\n' "$include_line"); then
      backup_file "$dropin_file"
      printf '%s\n' "$include_line" > "$dropin_file"
    fi
  fi

  if [[ -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]]; then
    if ! grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*Include[[:space:]]+/etc/ssh/sshd_config\.d/\*\.conf[[:space:]]*$' "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_D="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
BACKUP_DIR="/var/backups"
STAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
BACKUP_FILE="${BACKUP_DIR}/sshd_config.${STAMP}.bak"

mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

backup_file() {
  local f="$1"
  if [[ -f "$f" ]]; then
    cp -a -- "$f" "$BACKUP_FILE"
  fi
}

remove_mac_directives() {
  local f="$1"
  [[ -f "$f" ]] || return 0
  local tmp

  awk '
    BEGIN { IGNORECASE=1 }
    /^[[:space:]]*MACs[[:space:]]+/ { next }
    /^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*MACs[[:space:]]+/ { next }

  ' "$f" > "$tmp"
  if ! cmp -s "$f" "$tmp"; then
    backup_file "$f"
    cat "$tmp" > "$f"
  fi
  rm -f "$tmp"
}

remove_mac_from_dropins() {

      { print }
    ' "$f" > "$tmp"

    fi

        else
          printf '\nInclude /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf\n' >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"

  systemctl mask gssproxy.service 2>/dev/null || true

  apt-get -y autoremove --purge 2>/dev/null || true

  fi
fi

for path in \
  /etc/systemd/system/gssproxy.service \

  /usr/lib/systemd/system/gssproxy.service \

  fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

BANNER_TEXT="Authorized use only. All activity may be monitored and reported."
SCHEMA="org.gnome.login-screen"
KEY="banner-message-enable"
TEXTKEY="banner-message-text"
LOCKDIR="/etc/dconf/db/gdm.d"
PROFILE="/etc/dconf/profile/gdm"
LOCKFILE="${LOCKDIR}/locks/${SCHEMA}"

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

mkdir -p "${LOCKDIR}/locks"

if [[ ! -f "${PROFILE}" ]] || ! grep -qE '^\s*system-db:gdm\s*$' "${PROFILE}"; then

system-db:gdm
EOF
fi

[${SCHEMA}]
${KEY}=true
${TEXTKEY}='${BANNER_TEXT}'
EOF

cat > "${LOCKFILE}" <<EOF
/${SCHEMA}/${KEY}
/${SCHEMA}/${TEXTKEY}
EOF

dconf update

if command -v gsettings >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if [[ -n "${DISPLAY:-}" ]] || [[ -n "${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR:-}" ]]; then
    true
  fi
fi

exit 0

Write an idempotent bash remediation script that ensures this account-lockout enforcement is in place persistently.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

PAM_FILE="/etc/pam.d/common-auth"
LOCK_LINE='auth required pam_faillock.so preauth silent deny=5 unlock_time=900'
AUTHFAIL_LINE='auth [default=die] pam_faillock.so authfail deny=5 unlock_time=900'
ACCOUNT_LINE='account required pam_faillock.so'

backup_file() {
  local f="$1"
  if [[ -f "$f" && ! -f "${f}.bak" ]]; then
    cp -p -- "$f" "${f}.bak"
  fi
}

ensure_line_present() {
  local file="$1"
  local line="$2"
  grep -Fxq -- "$line" "$file" || printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$file"
}

remove_matching_lines() {
  local file="$1"
  local pattern="$2"
  local tmp
  tmp="$(mktemp)"
  grep -Ev -- "$pattern" "$file" > "$tmp"
  cat "$tmp" > "$file"
  rm -f "$tmp"
}

if [[ ! -f "$PAM_FILE" ]]; then
  exit 1
fi

backup_file "$PAM_FILE"

remove_matching_lines "$PAM_FILE" '^[[:space:]]*auth[[:space:]].*pam_faillock\.so.*(preauth|authfail|account)'
remove_matching_lines "$PAM_FILE" '^[[:space:]]*account[[:space:]].*pam_faillock\.so'

tmp="$(mktemp)"
awk -v lock="$LOCK_LINE" -v authfail="$AUTHFAIL_LINE" -v account="$ACCOUNT_LINE" '
BEGIN { inserted_lock=0; inserted_authfail=0; inserted_account=0 }
{
  print
  if (!inserted_lock && $1 == "auth" && $2 == "[success=1" && $3 == "default=ignore]" && $4 == "pam_unix.so") {
    print lock
    inserted_lock=1
  }
  if (!inserted_authfail && $1 == "auth" && $2 == "[success=1" && $3 == "default=ignore]" && $4 == "pam_unix.so") {
    print authfail
    inserted_authfail=1
  }
  if (!inserted_account && $1 == "account" && $2 == "required" && $3 == "pam_unix.so") {
    print account
    inserted_account=1
  }
}
END {
  if (!inserted_lock) print lock
  if (!inserted_authfail) print authfail
  if (!inserted_account) print account
}
' "$PAM_FILE" > "$tmp"
cat "$tmp" > "$PAM_FILE"
rm -f "$tmp"

ensure_line_present "$PAM_FILE" "$LOCK_LINE"
ensure_line_present "$PAM_FILE" "$AUTHFAIL_LINE"
ensure_line_present "$PAM_FILE" "$ACCOUNT_LINE"

exit 0
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

LOCK_FILE="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
PAM_COMMON_AUTH="/etc/pam.d/common-auth"
PAM_COMMON_ACCOUNT="/etc/pam.d/common-account"

backup_file() {
  local f="$1"
  if [[ -f "$f" && ! -f "${f}.bak" ]]; then
    cp -a "$f" "${f}.bak"
  fi
}

ensure_line_in_file() {
  local file="$1"
  local line="$2"
  grep -Fxq "$line" "$file" 2>/dev/null || printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$file"
}

backup_file "$LOCK_FILE"
backup_file "$PAM_COMMON_AUTH"
backup_file "$PAM_COMMON_ACCOUNT"

touch "$LOCK_FILE"

tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$tmpfile"' EXIT

{
  if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*deny[[:space:]]*=/) {
    print "deny = 3"

  } else if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*unlock_time[[:space:]]*=/) {

  } else if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*fail_interval[[:space:]]*=/) {

  } else if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*even_deny_root[[:space:]]*=/) {

  } else if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*root_unlock_time[[:space:]]*=/) {
    print "root_unlock_time = 0"
  } else {
    print
  }
}
END {
  if (!found) print "deny = 3"
}
' "$LOCK_FILE" > "$tmpfile"

grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*unlock_time[[:space:]]*=' "$LOCK_FILE" || echo "unlock_time = 0" >> "$LOCK_FILE"
grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*fail_interval[[:space:]]*=' "$LOCK_FILE" || echo "fail_interval = 900" >> "$LOCK_FILE"

fi

if ! grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*root_unlock_time[[:space:]]*=' "$LOCK_FILE"; then

fi

if [[ -f "$PAM_COMMON_AUTH" ]]; then

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

PAM_COMMON_AUTH="/etc/pam.d/common-auth"
PAM_COMMON_ACCOUNT="/etc/pam.d/common-account"
PAM_COMMON_AUTH_PREAUTH="/etc/pam.d/common-auth.preauth"
PAM_COMMON_ACCOUNT_PREAUTH="/etc/pam.d/common-account.preauth"
FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

backup_file() {
  local f="$1"
  [[ -e "$f" ]] || return 0
  cp -a -- "$f" "${f}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)" 2>/dev/null || true
}

ensure_line_in_file() {
  local file="$1"
  local line="$2"
  touch "$file"
  grep -Fxq -- "$line" "$file" || printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$file"
}

replace_or_append_block() {
  local file="$1"
  local start_marker="$2"
  local end_marker="$3"
  local content="$4"

  touch "$file"
  if grep -Fqx -- "$start_marker" "$file" && grep -Fqx -- "$end_marker" "$file"; then
    awk -v s="$start_marker" -v e="$end_marker" -v c="$content" '
      BEGIN { inblock=0 }
      $0==s { print; print c; inblock=1; next }
      $0==e { inblock=0; print; next }
      !inblock { print }
    ' "$file" > "${file}.tmp" && mv "${file}.tmp" "$file"
  else
    {
      printf '%s\n' "$start_marker"
      printf '%s\n' "$content"
      printf '%s\n' "$end_marker"
    } >> "$file"
  fi
}

backup_file "$PAM_COMMON_AUTH"
backup_file "$PAM_COMMON_ACCOUNT"
backup_file "$FAILLOCK_CONF"

touch "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*deny[[:space:]]*=' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
  sed -i -E 's/^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*deny[[:space:]]*=.*/deny = 5/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
else
  ensure_line_in_file "$FAILLOCK_CONF" "deny = 5"
fi

if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*unlock_time[[:space:]]*=' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
  sed -i -E 's/^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*unlock_time[[:space:]]*=.*/unlock_time = 900/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
else

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

files=(
  /etc/pam.d/common-auth
  /etc/pam.d/common-account
  /etc/pam.d/common-password
  /etc/pam.d/common-session
  /etc/pam.d/common-session-noninteractive
  /etc/pam.d/login
  /etc/pam.d/sshd
  /etc/pam.d/su
  /etc/pam.d/sudo
)

for f in "${files[@]}"; do
  [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue

  tmp="$(mktemp)"
  awk '
    BEGIN { inserted=0 }
    {
      if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*auth[[:space:]]+required[[:space:]]+pam_faillock\.so/ && $0 !~ /silent/) {
        sub(/$/, " silent")
      }
      if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*account[[:space:]]+required[[:space:]]+pam_faillock\.so/ && $0 !~ /silent/) {
        sub(/$/, " silent")
      }
      if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*password[[:space:]]+required[[:space:]]+pam_faillock\.so/ && $0 !~ /silent/) {
        sub(/$/, " silent")
      }
      if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*session[[:space:]]+required[[:space:]]+pam_faillock\.so/ && $0 !~ /silent/) {
        sub(/$/, " silent")
      }
      print
    }
  ' "$f" > "$tmp"

  if ! cmp -s "$f" "$tmp"; then
    cp -p "$tmp" "$f"
  fi
  rm -f "$tmp"
done

if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

MIN_ROUNDS=5000

update_file() {
  local file="$1"
  local tmp
  tmp="$(mktemp)"

  awk -v min="$MIN_ROUNDS" '
    BEGIN { found=0 }
    /^[[:space:]]*#/ { print; next }
    /^[[:space:]]*ENCRYPT_METHOD[[:space:]]*=/ {
      print "ENCRYPT_METHOD SHA512"
      next
    }
    /^[[:space:]]*SHA_CRYPT_MIN_ROUNDS[[:space:]]*=/ {

      found=1
      next
    }
    /^[[:space:]]*SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS[[:space:]]*=/ {
      print "SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS " min
      found=1
      next
    }
    { print }
    END {
      if (!found) {
        # handled by caller
      }
    }
  ' "$file" > "$tmp"

    printf '\nSHA_CRYPT_MIN_ROUNDS %s\n' "$MIN_ROUNDS" >> "$tmp"

  if ! grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS[[:space:]]*=' "$tmp"; then
    printf 'SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS %s\n' "$MIN_ROUNDS" >> "$tmp"
  fi
  if ! grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*ENCRYPT_METHOD[[:space:]]*=' "$tmp"; then
    printf 'ENCRYPT_METHOD SHA512\n' >> "$tmp"
  fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

unit="debug-shell.service"
unit_path="/etc/systemd/system/${unit}"
mask_target="/dev/null"

if [[ "${EUID:-$(id -u)}" -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system

if [[ -L "$unit_path" ]]; then
  current_target="$(readlink -f "$unit_path" || true)"
  if [[ "$current_target" != "$mask_target" ]]; then
    rm -f "$unit_path"
    ln -s "$mask_target" "$unit_path"
  fi
elif [[ -e "$unit_path" ]]; then

  ln -s "$mask_target" "$unit_path"

systemctl mask "$unit" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  systemctl is-enabled "$unit" >/dev/null 2>&1 && systemctl mask "$unit" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, configure the system's user resource limits so that any single account is restricted to no more than 10 concurrent login sessions, mitigating denial-of-service conditions caused by excessive or runaway simultaneous logins. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that enforces this per-user concurrent-session cap so it applies to all users and persists across reboots.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

LIMIT_VALUE="10"
LIMITS_FILE="/etc/security/limits.d/99-concurrent-sessions.conf"
PAM_FILE="/etc/pam.d/common-session"
PAM_NONINTERACTIVE_FILE="/etc/pam.d/common-session-noninteractive"

install -d -m 0755 /etc/security/limits.d

cat > "${LIMITS_FILE}" <<EOF
* hard maxlogins ${LIMIT_VALUE}
* soft maxlogins ${LIMIT_VALUE}
EOF
chmod 0644 "${LIMITS_FILE}"

ensure_pam_line() {
  local file="$1"
  local line="session required pam_limits.so"

  [[ -f "$file" ]] || return 0

  if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*session[[:space:]]+required[[:space:]]+pam_limits\.so([[:space:]]|$)' "$file"; then
    return 0
  fi

  if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*session[[:space:]]+.*pam_limits\.so([[:space:]]|$)' "$file"; then
    return 0
  fi

  printf '\n%s\n' "$line" >> "$file"
}

ensure_pam_line "$PAM_FILE"
ensure_pam_line "$PAM_NONINTERACTIVE_FILE"

prompt:
On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, ensure that every file and directory within each local interactive user's home directory is group-owned by a group of which that user is a member, so that files cannot be inadvertently exposed to unrelated users through an inappropriate group ownership. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that corrects the group ownership of all such files and directories for all local interactive users.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

if [[ ${EUID:-$(id -u)} -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

is_local_user() {
  local user="$1"
  getent passwd "$user" >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 1
  local pwline shell uid gid home
  pwline="$(getent passwd "$user")"
  IFS=: read -r _ _ uid gid _ home shell <<<"$pwline"

  [[ "$uid" -ge 1000 ]] || return 1
  [[ "$shell" != "/usr/sbin/nologin" && "$shell" != "/bin/false" ]] || return 1
  [[ -d "$home" ]] || return 1

  case "$home" in
    /home/*) ;;
    *) return 1 ;;
  esac

  [[ "$user" != "nobody" ]] || return 1
  return 0
}

user_groups() {
  local user="$1"
  id -Gn "$user" 2>/dev/null || true
}

for user in $(getent passwd | awk -F: '$3 >= 1000 && $7 != "/usr/sbin/nologin" && $7 != "/bin/false" {print $1}'); do
  if ! is_local_user "$user"; then
    continue
  fi

  home_dir="$(getent passwd "$user" | awk -F: '{print $6}')"
  [[ -d "$home_dir" ]] || continue

  mapfile -t groups < <(user_groups "$user" | tr ' ' '\n' | awk 'NF')
  if [[ ${#groups[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
    continue
  fi

  find "$home_dir" -xdev \( -type f -o -type d -o -type l \) -print0 |
    while IFS= read -r -d '' path; do
      current_group="$(stat -c '%G' "$path" 2>/dev/null || true)"
      if [[ -z "$current_group" ]]; then
        continue
      fi

      if printf '%s\n' "${groups[@]}" | grep -Fxq -- "$current_group"; then
        continue
      fi

      target_group="$user"
      if ! getent group "$target_group" >/dev/null 2>&1 || ! id -nG "$user" | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -Fxq -- "$target_group"; then
        target_group="$(id -gn "$user")"
      fi

      if getent group "$target_group" >/dev/null 2>&1 && id -nG "$user" | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -Fxq -- "$target_group"; then
        chgrp -h "$target_group" "$path"
      fi
    done
done

Overly permissive home-directory contents allow other users to read or modify another user's files. Files and directories whose names begin with a dot are exempt. #!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

while IFS=: read -r user _ uid gid gecos home shell; do
  [[ -n "${user}" ]] || continue
  [[ "${uid}" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || continue
  [[ "${uid}" -ge 1000 ]] || continue
  [[ "${uid}" -ne 65534 ]] || continue
  [[ "${shell}" != */nologin && "${shell}" != */false ]] || continue
  [[ -d "${home}" ]] || continue

    while IFS= read -r -d '' path; do
      chmod go-w "${path}" 2>/dev/null || true
      if [[ -d "${path}" ]]; then
        chmod go+x "${path}" 2>/dev/null || true
      fi
    done
done < /etc/passwd

Write an idempotent bash remediation script that ensures every interactive user's local initialization files (for all human users with home directories as well as the root account) are no more permissive than owner read/write/execute plus group read only (octal 0740), tightening any that exceed this without loosening any that are already stricter.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

max_mode=0740

is_human_user() {
  local user="$1" uid="$2" shell="$3" home="$4"
  [[ "$uid" -ge 1000 ]] || [[ "$user" == "root" ]]
  [[ -n "$home" ]] && [[ "$home" != "/" ]]
  [[ "$shell" != "/usr/sbin/nologin" ]] && [[ "$shell" != "/bin/false" ]]
}

tighten_file() {
  local file="$1"
  [[ -e "$file" ]] || return 0
  [[ -f "$file" || -L "$file" ]] || return 0
  local current_mode
  current_mode="$(stat -c '%a' -- "$file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  [[ -n "$current_mode" ]] || return 0
  if (( 8#$current_mode > 8#$max_mode )); then
    chmod 0740 -- "$file"
  fi
}

process_home() {
  local home="$1"
  local f
  for f in \
    "$home/.bashrc" \
    "$home/.bash_profile" \
    "$home/.bash_login" \
    "$home/.profile" \
    "$home/.zshrc" \
    "$home/.zprofile" \
    "$home/.zlogin" \
    "$home/.kshrc" \
    "$home/.cshrc" \
    "$home/.tcshrc" \
    "$home/.login"
  do
    tighten_file "$f"
  done
}

while IFS=: read -r user _ uid _ _ home shell; do
  if is_human_user "$user" "$uid" "$shell" "$home"; then
    process_home "$home"
  fi
done < /etc/passwd

exit 0

On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, the default file-creation permission mask for users of the Bash shell may be too permissive, allowing newly created files to be accessible by group or other users and risking unauthorized disclosure or modification. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that sets the default Bash shell file-creation mask system-wide to 027, so that newly created files deny write access to group members and all access to others by default, ensuring the setting persists across reboots and new login sessions.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

TARGET_MASK='027'
PROFILE_FILE='/etc/profile'
MARKER_BEGIN='# BEGIN MANAGED BY UMASK REMEDIATION'
MARKER_END='# END MANAGED BY UMASK REMEDIATION'

tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$tmpfile"' EXIT

if [[ -f "$PROFILE_FILE" ]]; then
  awk -v begin="$MARKER_BEGIN" -v end="$MARKER_END" '
    $0 == begin {skip=1; next}
    $0 == end {skip=0; next}
    !skip {print}
  ' "$PROFILE_FILE" > "$tmpfile"
else
  : > "$tmpfile"
fi

cat >> "$tmpfile" <<EOF
$MARKER_BEGIN
# Set default Bash umask system-wide for login shells.
umask $TARGET_MASK
$MARKER_END
EOF

install -m 0644 "$tmpfile" "$PROFILE_FILE"

response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

if [[ ${EUID:-$(id -u)} -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

disable_vsyscall_param="vsyscall=none"
grub_default="/etc/default/grub"
grub_d_dir="/etc/default/grub.d"
grub_d_file="${grub_d_dir}/99-disable-vsyscall.cfg"

ensure_param_in_line() {
  local line="$1"
  local param="$2"
  if [[ "$line" =~ (^|[[:space:]])${param}([[:space:]]|$) ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$line"
  else
    if [[ -n "$line" ]]; then
      printf '%s %s\n' "$line" "$param"
    else
      printf '%s\n' "$param"
    fi
  fi
}

update_grub_file() {
  local file="$1"
  [[ -f "$file" ]] || return 0

  local tmp
  tmp="$(mktemp)"
  awk -v param="$disable_vsyscall_param" '
    BEGIN { updated=0 }
    /^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX(_DEFAULT)?=/ {
      line=$0
      sub(/^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="/, "", line)
      sub(/^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="/, "", line)
      sub(/"$/, "", line)
      if (line !~ "(^|[[:space:]])" param "([[:space:]]|$)") {
        if (length(line) > 0) line = line " " param
        else line = param
      }
      if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT=/) {
        print "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT=\"" line "\""
      } else {
        print "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"" line "\""
      }
      updated=1
      next
    }
    { print }
    END {
      if (!updated) {
        print "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT=\"" param "\""
      }
    }
  ' "$file" > "$tmp"
  install -m 0644 "$tmp" "$file"
  rm -f "$tmp"
}

mkdir -p "$grub_d_dir"

cat > "$grub_d_file" <<EOF
# Managed by remediation script: disable legacy vsyscall for all kernels
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="\${GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT} ${disable_vsyscall_param}"
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="\${GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX} ${disable_vsyscall_param}"
EOF
chmod 0644 "$grub_d_file"

update_grub_file "$grub_default"

if command -v update-grub >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  update-grub
elif command -v grub-mkconfig >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  grub_mkconfig -o /boot/grub/grub.cfg
fi
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

CONF_FILE="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
DROPIN_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${DROPIN_DIR}/60-tls-default-stream.conf"

mkdir -p "$DROPIN_DIR"

if ! grep -RqsE '^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver\s+gtls\s*$' "$CONF_FILE" "$DROPIN_DIR" 2>/dev/null; then
  if grep -qE '^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver\s+' "$CONF_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    sed -i -E 's/^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver\s+.*/$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls/' "$CONF_FILE"
  else
    printf '\n$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls\n' >> "$CONF_FILE"
  fi
fi

if ! grep -RqsE '^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriverMode\s+1\s*$' "$CONF_FILE" "$DROPIN_DIR" 2>/dev/null; then
  if grep -qE '^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriverMode\s+' "$CONF_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    sed -i -E 's/^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriverMode\s+.*/$DefaultNetstreamDriverMode 1/' "$CONF_FILE"
  else
    printf '\n$DefaultNetstreamDriverMode 1\n' >> "$CONF_FILE"
  fi
fi

if ! grep -RqsE '^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriverCAFile\s+' "$CONF_FILE" "$DROPIN_DIR" 2>/dev/null; then
  if [ ! -f "$DROPIN_FILE" ]; then
    cat > "$DROPIN_FILE" <<'EOF'
$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls
$DefaultNetstreamDriverMode 1
EOF
  else
    grep -qE '^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver\s+gtls\s*$' "$DROPIN_FILE" || printf '\n$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls\n' >> "$DROPIN_FILE"
    grep -qE '^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriverMode\s+1\s*$' "$DROPIN_FILE" || printf '$DefaultNetstreamDriverMode 1\n' >> "$DROPIN_FILE"
  fi
fi

if systemctl list-unit-files rsyslog.service >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  systemctl enable rsyslog.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  systemctl restart rsyslog.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl start rsyslog.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi
```

On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, ensure that the system logging service is configured to capture and record all authentication, privileged-authentication, and system daemon activity to persistent log files, so that remote access attempts and related events are monitored for intrusion detection and compliance. Specifically, authentication and privileged-authentication messages must be routed to the secure log, and daemon messages to the general system message log. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that establishes this logging configuration and ensures it remains in effect across reboots.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  exec sudo -n bash "$0" "$@"
fi

mkdir -p /etc/rsyslog.d /var/log

cat >/etc/rsyslog.d/50-auth-daemon.conf <<'EOF'
auth,authpriv.*                 /var/log/secure
daemon.*                        /var/log/messages
EOF

touch /var/log/secure /var/log/messages
chmod 0600 /var/log/secure
chmod 0644 /var/log/messages
chown root:adm /var/log/secure /var/log/messages || true

if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  systemctl enable rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  systemctl restart rsyslog
else
  service rsyslog restart
fi

exit 0
```

On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, prevent all IPv6 network interfaces from accepting router advertisement messages, since a forged advertisement could let an attacker reroute traffic and perform a man-in-the-middle attack. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that disables acceptance of IPv6 router advertisements across all interfaces, applying the change at runtime and ensuring it persists across reboots.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SYSCTL_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-ipv6-ra.conf"

cat > "$SYSCTL_FILE" <<'EOF'
net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra = 0
net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra = 0
EOF

sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra=0 >/dev/null
sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra=0 >/dev/null

while IFS= read -r -d '' iface; do
  sysctl_key="net.ipv6.conf.${iface}.accept_ra"
  if sysctl -a 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${sysctl_key} = "; then
    sysctl -w "${sysctl_key}=0" >/dev/null || true
  fi
done < <(find /proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -printf '%f\0')

sysctl --system >/dev/null
```

prompt:
On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, the kernel currently accepts IPv6 ICMP redirect messages on all interfaces, which an attacker could abuse to alter routing and stage a man-in-the-middle attack. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that configures the kernel's IPv6 networking so that ICMP redirect messages are not accepted on any interface, applying the change at runtime and ensuring it persists across reboots.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SYSCTL_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-ipv6-icmp-redirects.conf"

apply_runtime() {
  sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects=0 >/dev/null
  sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects=0 >/dev/null
  sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.lo.accept_redirects=0 >/dev/null 2>/dev/null || true

  while IFS= read -r iface; do
    [[ -n "${iface}" ]] || continue
    sysctl -w "net.ipv6.conf.${iface}.accept_redirects=0" >/dev/null 2>/dev/null || true
  done < <(find /proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -printf '%f\n' 2>/dev/null || true)
}

ensure_persistent() {
  install -d -m 0755 /etc/sysctl.d
  cat > "${SYSCTL_FILE}" <<'EOF'
net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
EOF
  chmod 0644 "${SYSCTL_FILE}"
}

apply_runtime
ensure_persistent
sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
```

SYSCTL_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-ipv4-icmp-redirects.conf"
KEY="net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects"
VALUE="0"

apply_runtime() {
  sysctl -w "${KEY}=${VALUE}" >/dev/null
}

ensure_persistent() {
  local tmp
  tmp="$(mktemp)"
  if [[ -f "${SYSCTL_FILE}" ]]; then
    grep -vE "^[[:space:]]*${KEY}[[:space:]]*=" "${SYSCTL_FILE}" > "${tmp}" || true
  fi
  printf '%s = %s\n' "${KEY}" "${VALUE}" >> "${tmp}"
  install -m 0644 "${tmp}" "${SYSCTL_FILE}"
  rm -f "${tmp}"
}

apply_runtime
ensure_persistent
sysctl --system >/dev/null
apply_runtime

response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

MODULE="atm"
CONF_FILE="/etc/modprobe.d/disable-${MODULE}.conf"
BLACKLIST_FILE="/etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-${MODULE}.conf"
INSTALL_FILE="/etc/modprobe.d/install-${MODULE}.conf"
SYSTEMD_DIR="/etc/systemd/system"
SERVICE_NAME="disable-${MODULE}.service"
SERVICE_PATH="${SYSTEMD_DIR}/${SERVICE_NAME}"

mkdir -p /etc/modprobe.d

cat > "${CONF_FILE}" <<'EOF'
# Prevent ATM kernel module from being loaded
blacklist atm
install atm /bin/false
EOF

cat > "${BLACKLIST_FILE}" <<'EOF'
# Additional blacklist for ATM kernel module
blacklist atm
EOF

cat > "${INSTALL_FILE}" <<'EOF'
# Block manual on-demand loading of ATM kernel module
install atm /bin/false
EOF

if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  cat > "${SERVICE_PATH}" <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Disable ATM kernel module loading
DefaultDependencies=no
Before=systemd-modules-load.service

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/usr/bin/true

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF
  systemctl daemon-reload >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if lsmod | awk '{print $1}' | grep -qx "${MODULE}"; then
  modprobe -r "${MODULE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if command -v update-initramfs >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  update-initramfs -u >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0
```

prompt:
On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, the IEEE 1394 (FireWire) high-speed serial bus support presents an attack surface through potential flaws in its implementation and via direct-memory-access risks. Configure the system so that FireWire support cannot be loaded or used: the relevant kernel driver must be prevented from loading and from being activated on demand. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that enforces this persistently across reboots.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

MODPROBE_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
BLACKLIST_FILE="${MODPROBE_DIR}/firewire-disable.conf"
MODULES_LOAD_DIR="/etc/modules-load.d"
MODULES_LOAD_FILE="${MODULES_LOAD_DIR}/firewire-disable.conf"
UDEV_RULES_DIR="/etc/udev/rules.d"
UDEV_RULES_FILE="${UDEV_RULES_DIR}/99-firewire-disable.rules"

mkdir -p "$MODPROBE_DIR" "$MODULES_LOAD_DIR" "$UDEV_RULES_DIR"

cat > "$BLACKLIST_FILE" <<'EOF'
blacklist firewire-core
blacklist firewire-ohci
blacklist firewire-sbp2
blacklist firewire-net
blacklist ohci1394
blacklist sbp2
blacklist raw1394
blacklist dv1394
blacklist ieee1394
blacklist ohci1394_legacy
EOF

cat > "$MODULES_LOAD_FILE" <<'EOF'
# Prevent FireWire-related modules from being loaded at boot
EOF

cat > "$UDEV_RULES_FILE" <<'EOF'
# Prevent FireWire devices from being activated on demand
ACTION=="add", SUBSYSTEM=="firewire", OPTIONS+="ignore_device"
ACTION=="add", SUBSYSTEM=="ieee1394", OPTIONS+="ignore_device"
EOF

if command -v update-initramfs >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  update-initramfs -u
fi

if command -v dracut >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  dracut -f
fi

for mod in firewire-core firewire-ohci firewire-sbp2 firewire-net ohci1394 sbp2 raw1394 dv1394 ieee1394 ohci1394_legacy; do
  if lsmod | awk '{print $1}' | grep -qx "$mod"; then
    modprobe -r "$mod" || true
  fi
done

if command -v udevadm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  udevadm control --reload-rules || true
  udevadm trigger --subsystem-match=firewire || true
  udevadm trigger --subsystem-match=ieee1394 || true
fi

exit 0
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash

On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, the Transparent Inter-Process Communication (TIPC) protocol provides messaging between nodes in a cluster but is rarely needed and increases the kernel's attack surface; any flaw in its implementation could be exploited. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that ensures the TIPC networking protocol support cannot be loaded or used on this system, with the configuration persisting across reboots.

response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

MODPROBE_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
BLACKLIST_FILE="${MODPROBE_DIR}/disable-tipc.conf"
SYSCTL_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-tipc.conf"

mkdir -p "$MODPROBE_DIR"

cat > "$BLACKLIST_FILE" <<'EOF'
blacklist tipc
install tipc /bin/false
EOF

cat > "$SYSCTL_FILE" <<'EOF'
net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1
EOF

if lsmod | awk '{print $1}' | grep -qx 'tipc'; then
  modprobe -r tipc || true
fi

if command -v modprobe >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  modprobe -r tipc 2>/dev/null || true
fi

if command -v sysctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if [ -d /sys/module/tipc ]; then
  echo "TIPC module is currently loaded and could not be unloaded automatically." >&2
  exit 1
fi

exit 0

response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

modprobe_d_dir="/etc/modprobe.d"
blacklist_file="${modprobe_d_dir}/99-disable-bluetooth.conf"
systemd_rfkill_dir="/etc/systemd/system/systemd-rfkill.service.d"
systemd_rfkill_dropin="${systemd_rfkill_dir}/disable-bluetooth.conf"

mkdir -p "$modprobe_d_dir" "$systemd_rfkill_dir"

cat > "$blacklist_file" <<'EOF'
blacklist bluetooth
blacklist btusb
blacklist btrtl
blacklist btintel
blacklist btbcm
blacklist btqca
blacklist btsdio
blacklist btmtk
install bluetooth /bin/false
install btusb /bin/false
install btrtl /bin/false
install btintel /bin/false
install btbcm /bin/false
install btqca /bin/false
install btsdio /bin/false
install btmtk /bin/false
EOF

cat > "$systemd_rfkill_dropin" <<'EOF'
[Service]
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/bin/true
EOF

if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl daemon-reload || true
    systemctl mask bluetooth.service bluetooth.target 2>/dev/null || true
    systemctl disable bluetooth.service bluetooth.target 2>/dev/null || true
    systemctl restart systemd-rfkill.service 2>/dev/null || true
fi

if command -v modprobe >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    modprobe -r bluetooth btusb btrtl btintel btbcm btqca btsdio btmtk 2>/dev/null || true
fi

if command -v update-initramfs >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    update-initramfs -u >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, the boot partition should never need to honor set-user-ID or set-group-ID permission bits, and allowing them there expands the attack surface for privilege escalation. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that ensures the boot partition is mounted in a way that prevents the operating system from honoring SUID/SGID bits on files stored there, with the setting taking effect on the running system and persisting across reboots. If the boot partition is not a separately mounted filesystem, the script should handle that gracefully.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

BOOT_MOUNTPOINT="/boot"
FSTAB_FILE="/etc/fstab"

is_mounted() {
  findmnt -rn "$BOOT_MOUNTPOINT" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

is_separate_mount() {
  findmnt -rn "$BOOT_MOUNTPOINT" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

get_mount_source() {
  findmnt -rn -o SOURCE --target "$BOOT_MOUNTPOINT" 2>/dev/null || true
}

get_mount_fstype() {
  findmnt -rn -o FSTYPE --target "$BOOT_MOUNTPOINT" 2>/dev/null || true
}

ensure_mount_option_nosuid() {
  local target="$1"
  local opts current_opts new_opts
  opts="$(findmnt -rn -o OPTIONS --target "$target" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  [[ -z "$opts" ]] && return 0

  IFS=',' read -r -a current_opts <<< "$opts"
  new_opts=()
  local found_nosuid=0
  local found_suid=0

  for opt in "${current_opts[@]}"; do
    case "$opt" in
      nosuid)
        found_nosuid=1
        new_opts+=("$opt")
        ;;
      suid)
        found_suid=1
        ;;
      *)
        new_opts+=("$opt")
        ;;
    esac
  done

  if [[ "$found_nosuid" -eq 0 ]]; then
    new_opts+=("nosuid")
  fi

  if [[ "$found_suid" -eq 1 || "$found_nosuid" -eq 0 ]]; then
    mount -o remount,"$(IFS=,; echo "${new_opts[*]}")" "$target"
  fi
}

update_fstab_for_boot() {
  local boot_src boot_fstype line tmpfile found=0
  boot_src="$(findmnt -rn -o SOURCE --target "$BOOT_MOUNTPOINT" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  boot_fstype="$(findmnt -rn -o FSTYPE --target "$BOOT_MOUNTPOINT" 2>/dev/null || true)"

  [[ -z "$boot_src" || -z "$boot_fstype" ]] && return 0

  tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
  trap 'rm -f "$tmpfile"' RETURN

  if [[ -f "$FSTAB_FILE" ]]; then
    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
      if [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# || -z "${line//[[:space:]]/}" ]]; then
        printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$tmpfile"
        continue
      fi

      local fs_spec fs_file fs_vfstype fs_mntops fs_freq fs_passno
      read -r fs_spec fs_file fs_vfstype fs_mntops fs_freq fs_passno <<< "$line" || true

      if [[ "$fs_file" == "$BOOT_MOUNTPOINT" ]]; then
        found=1
        fs_mntops="${fs_mntops:-defaults}"
        if [[ "$fs_mntops" != *nosuid* ]]; then
          if [[ "$fs_mntops" == "defaults" ]]; then
            fs_mntops="defaults,nosuid"
          else
            fs_mntops="${fs_mntops},nosuid"
          fi
        fi
        printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' "$fs_spec" "$fs_file" "$fs_vfstype" "$fs_mntops" "${fs_freq:-0}" "${fs_passno:-0}" >> "$tmpfile"
      else
        printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$tmpfile"
      fi
    done < "$FSTAB_FILE"
  fi

  if [[ "$found" -eq 0 ]]; then
    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' "$boot_src" "$BOOT_MOUNTPOINT" "$boot_fstype" "defaults,nosuid" "0" "2" >> "$tmpfile"
  fi

  install -m 0644 "$tmpfile" "$FSTAB_FILE"
}

if is_mounted; then
  ensure_mount_option_nosuid "$BOOT_MOUNTPOINT"
  update_fstab_for_boot
else
  if grep -Eq "^[^#].*[[:space:]]/boot[[:space:]]" "$FSTAB_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    update_fstab_for_boot
  fi
fi

exit 0

On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, the shared-memory temporary filesystem mounted at /dev/shm should not permit the interpretation of device files, since legitimate character and block device files belong only under the system device directory and allowing them in a world-writable temporary area could be abused to bypass access controls. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that ensures the /dev/shm filesystem disallows device-file interpretation, applied immediately and persistently across reboots.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

mountpoint="/dev/shm"
fstab="/etc/fstab"
tmpfile="$(mktemp)"

cleanup() {
  rm -f "$tmpfile"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

apply_runtime() {
  if mountpoint -q "$mountpoint"; then
    if findmnt -no OPTIONS "$mountpoint" | tr ',' '\n' | grep -qx 'nodev'; then
      return 0
    fi
    mount -o remount,nodev "$mountpoint"
  fi
}

ensure_fstab() {
  local found=0
  local changed=0

  : > "$tmpfile"

  while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
    if [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] || [[ -z "${line//[[:space:]]/}" ]]; then
      printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$tmpfile"
      continue
    fi

    read -r fs mp type opts dump pass <<<"$line" || true

    if [[ "${mp:-}" == "$mountpoint" ]]; then
      found=1
      if [[ "${opts:-}" != *nodev* ]]; then
        if [[ -n "${opts:-}" ]]; then
          opts="${opts},nodev"
        else
          opts="nodev"
        fi
        changed=1
      fi
      printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' "${fs:-}" "${mp:-}" "${type:-}" "${opts:-}" "${dump:-0}" "${pass:-0}" >> "$tmpfile"
    else
      printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$tmpfile"
    fi
  done < "$fstab"

  if [[ "$found" -eq 0 ]]; then
    printf 'tmpfs\t/dev/shm\ttmpfs\tdefaults,nodev,nosuid,noexec\t0\t0\n' >> "$tmpfile"
    changed=1
  fi

  if [[ "$changed" -eq 1 ]]; then
    cp -f "$tmpfile" "$fstab"
  fi
}

apply_runtime
ensure_fstab
apply_runtime

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

FSTAB_FILE="/etc/fstab"
MOUNTPOINT="/dev/shm"
OPTIONS="defaults,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,size=50%"
TMPFILE="$(mktemp)"

cleanup() {
  rm -f "$TMPFILE"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

if mountpoint -q "$MOUNTPOINT"; then
  if ! findmnt -no OPTIONS "$MOUNTPOINT" | tr ',' '\n' | grep -qx 'noexec'; then
    mount -o remount,noexec,nosuid,nodev "$MOUNTPOINT"
  fi
fi

if [[ -f "$FSTAB_FILE" ]]; then
  awk -v mp="$MOUNTPOINT" -v opts="$OPTIONS" '
    BEGIN { found=0 }
    $0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*#/ { print; next }
    NF >= 4 && $2 == mp && $3 == "tmpfs" {
      $4 = opts
      found=1
      print
      next
    }
    { print }
    END {
      if (!found) {
        print "tmpfs " mp " tmpfs " opts " 0 0"
      }
    }
  ' "$FSTAB_FILE" > "$TMPFILE"
  if ! cmp -s "$FSTAB_FILE" "$TMPFILE"; then
    cp -p "$FSTAB_FILE" "${FSTAB_FILE}.bak.$(date +%s)"
    cat "$TMPFILE" > "$FSTAB_FILE"
  fi
else
  printf '%s\n' "tmpfs $MOUNTPOINT tmpfs $OPTIONS 0 0" > "$FSTAB_FILE"
fi

if mountpoint -q "$MOUNTPOINT"; then
  if ! findmnt -no OPTIONS "$MOUNTPOINT" | tr ',' '\n' | grep -qx 'noexec'; then
    mount -o remount,noexec,nosuid,nodev "$MOUNTPOINT"
  fi
fi

Configure this shared-memory mount so that the setuid and setgid bits on any executables stored there are ignored, and ensure this protection is active immediately and persists across reboots. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that enforces this.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.

On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, ensure that every non-root local filesystem is mounted in a way that prevents any files on it from being interpreted as character or block device nodes, since legitimate device files should exist only under the root partition's device directory (or within purpose-built chroot jails). Permitting device-node interpretation on other partitions could let an attacker plant rogue device files to gain unauthorized access to underlying hardware or memory. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that applies this protection to all non-root local partitions so that it persists across reboots.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

if [[ ${EUID:-$(id -u)} -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

FSTAB="/etc/fstab"
BACKUP="${FSTAB}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
cp -a "$FSTAB" "$BACKUP"

tmp="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT

awk '
BEGIN { OFS="\t" }
{
  raw=$0
  line=$0
  sub(/[[:space:]]*#.*/, "", line)
  if (line ~ /^[[:space:]]*$/) { print raw; next }

  n=split(line, f, /[[:space:]]+/)
  if (n < 4) { print raw; next }

  spec=f[1]; mnt=f[2]; type=f[3]; opts=f[4]
  if (mnt == "/" || spec ~ /^#/ || spec ~ /^UUID=/ || spec ~ /^LABEL=/ || spec ~ /^PARTUUID=/ || spec ~ /^PARTLABEL=/ || spec ~ /^\/dev\//) {
    if (opts !~ /(^|,)nodev(,|$)/) {
      if (opts == "defaults" || opts == "rw" || opts == "ro" || opts == "relatime" || opts == "auto" || opts == "noauto" || opts == "") {
        opts = (opts == "" ? "nodev" : opts ",nodev")
      } else {
        opts = opts ",nodev"
      }
      f[4]=opts
      out=f[1]
      for (i=2; i<=n; i++) out = out OFS f[i]
      print out
      next
    }
  }
  print raw
}
' "$FSTAB" > "$tmp"

if ! cmp -s "$FSTAB" "$tmp"; then
  cat "$tmp" > "$FSTAB"
fi

mountpoint -q / || true

while IFS= read -r line; do
  [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
  [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue
  set -- $line
  spec="${1:-}"
  mnt="${2:-}"
  type="${3:-}"
  opts="${4:-}"
  [[ -z "$spec" || -z "$mnt" || -z "$type" ]] && continue
  [[ "$mnt" == "/" ]] && continue
  if [[ "$spec" == /dev/* || "$spec" == UUID=* || "$spec" == LABEL=* || "$spec" == PARTUUID=* || "$spec" == PARTLABEL=* ]]; then
    if mountpoint -q "$mnt"; then
      current_opts="$(findmnt -no OPTIONS --target "$mnt" 2>/dev/null || true)"
      if [[ ",$current_opts," != *",nodev,"* ]]; then
        mount -o remount,nodev "$mnt" 2>/dev/null || true
      fi
    fi
  fi
done < <(grep -Ev '^[[:space:]]*#|^[[:space:]]*$' "$FSTAB")

exit 0

cp -a "$FSTAB" "$BACKUP"

tmp="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT

awk '
BEGIN { OFS="\t" }
{
  raw=$0
  line=$0
  sub(/[[:space:]]*#.*/, "", line)
  if (line ~ /^[[:space:]]*$/) { print raw; next }

  n=split(line, f, /[[:space:]]+/)
  if (n < 4) { print raw; next }

  spec=f[1]; mnt=f[2]; type=f[3]; opts=f[4]
  if (mnt == "/" || spec ~ /^#/ || spec ~ /^UUID=/ || spec ~ /^LABEL=/ || spec ~ /^PARTUUID=/ || spec ~ /^PARTLABEL=/ || spec ~ /^\/dev\//) {
    if (opts !~ /(^|,)nodev(,|$)/) {
      if (opts == "defaults" || opts == "rw" || opts == "ro" || opts == "relatime" || opts == "auto" || opts == "noauto" || opts == "") {
        opts = (opts == "" ? "nodev" : opts ",nodev")
      } else {
        opts = opts ",nodev"
      }
      f[4]=opts
      out=f[1]
      for (i=2; i<=n; i++) out = out OFS f[i]
      print out
      next
    }
  }
  print raw
}
' "$FSTAB" > "$tmp"

if ! cmp -s "$FSTAB" "$tmp"; then
  cat "$tmp" > "$FSTAB"
fi

mountpoint -q / || true

while IFS= read -r line; do
  [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
  [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue
  set -- $line
  spec="${1:-}"
  mnt="${2:-}"
  type="${3:-}"
  opts="${4:-}"
  [[ -z "$spec" || -z "$mnt" || -z "$type" ]] && continue
  [[ "$mnt" == "/" ]] && continue
  if [[ "$spec" == /dev/* || "$spec" == UUID=* || "$spec" == LABEL=* || "$spec" == PARTUUID=* || "$spec" == PARTLABEL=* ]]; then
    if mountpoint -q "$mnt"; then
      current_opts="$(findmnt -no OPTIONS --target "$mnt" 2>/dev/null || true)"
      if [[ ",$current_opts," != *",nodev,"* ]]; then
        mount -o remount,nodev "$mnt" 2>/dev/null || true
      fi
    fi
  fi
done < <(grep -Ev '^[[:space:]]*#|^[[:space:]]*$' "$FSTAB")

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

MODPROBE_CONF_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
BLACKLIST_FILE="${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}/disable-usb-webcam.conf"
INSTALL_FILE="${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}/disable-usb-webcam-install.conf"

mkdir -p "${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}"

cat > "${BLACKLIST_FILE}" <<'EOF'
blacklist uvcvideo

if command -v update-initramfs >/dev/null 2>&1; then

fi

exit 0

Configure the system so that core dumps are not written to disk, and ensure this behavior takes effect immediately and persists across reboots. response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

disable_systemd_coredump() {
  local cfg_dir="/etc/systemd/coredump.conf.d"
  local cfg_file="${cfg_dir}/99-disable-coredumps.conf"

  mkdir -p "$cfg_dir"
  cat > "$cfg_file" <<'EOF'
[Coredump]
Storage=none
ProcessSizeMax=0
ExternalSizeMax=0
EOF

  chmod 0644 "$cfg_file"
}

disable_core_pattern() {
  local sysctl_dir="/etc/sysctl.d"
  local sysctl_file="${sysctl_dir}/99-disable-core-dumps.conf"

  mkdir -p "$sysctl_dir"
  cat > "$sysctl_file" <<'EOF'
fs.suid_dumpable = 0
kernel.core_pattern = |/bin/false
EOF

  chmod 0644 "$sysctl_file"
}

apply_runtime_settings() {
  sysctl -q -w fs.suid_dumpable=0 >/dev/null || true
  sysctl -q -w kernel.core_pattern='|/bin/false' >/dev/null || true

  if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl daemon-reload >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    systemctl restart systemd-coredump.socket >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    systemctl restart systemd-coredump.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  fi
}

disable_systemd_coredump
disable_core_pattern
apply_runtime_settings

exit 0
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SYSCTL_KEY="kernel.kexec_load_disabled"
SYSCTL_VALUE="1"
SYSCTL_CONF_DIR="/etc/sysctl.d"
SYSCTL_CONF_FILE="${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}/99-disable-kexec-load.conf"

apply_runtime() {
  if [[ -w /proc/sys/kernel/kexec_load_disabled ]]; then
    current="$(cat /proc/sys/kernel/kexec_load_disabled 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [[ "${current}" != "${SYSCTL_VALUE}" ]]; then
      sysctl -w "${SYSCTL_KEY}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >/dev/null
    fi
  fi
}

persist_setting() {
  mkdir -p "${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}"
  if [[ -f "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]]; then
    if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_KEY}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"; then
      tmp="$(mktemp)"
      awk -v key="${SYSCTL_KEY}" '
        BEGIN { found=0 }
        $0 ~ "^[[:space:]]*" key "[[:space:]]*=" { next }
        { print }
      ' "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" > "${tmp}"
      printf '%s = %s\n' "${SYSCTL_KEY}" "${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >> "${tmp}"
      install -m 0644 "${tmp}" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
      rm -f "${tmp}"
    fi
  else
    printf '%s = %s\n' "${SYSCTL_KEY}" "${SYSCTL_VALUE}" > "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
  fi
}

apply_runtime
persist_setting

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash

LIMITS_FILE="/etc/security/limits.conf"

LIMITS_D_FILE="${LIMITS_D_DIR}/99-core-dumps.conf"

The setting must be present on the boot loader command line for all currently installed kernels and must also be applied automatically to any kernels installed in the future, so the protection persists across reboots and upgrades. Write an idempotent bash remediation script that achieves this end state.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.
response:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

PARAM="page_poison=1"
GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE="/etc/default/grub"
GRUB_D_FILE="/etc/default/grub.d/99-page-poison.cfg"

ensure_param_in_cmdline() {
  local line="$1"
  if [[ "$line" =~ (^|[[:space:]])${PARAM}($|[[:space:]]) ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$line"
  else
    if [[ -n "$line" ]]; then
      printf '%s %s\n' "$line" "$PARAM"
    else
      printf '%s\n' "$PARAM"
    fi
  fi
}

update_grub_cfg_file() {
  local file="$1"
  local current cmdline

  if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
    current="$(cat "$file")"
  else
    current=""
  fi

  if grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX(_DEFAULT)?=' <<<"$current"; then
    cmdline="$(awk -F= '
      BEGIN{OFS="="}
      /^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT=/ {
        sub(/^[^=]*="/,"",$0); sub(/"$/,"",$0); print $0
      }
      /^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=/ {
        sub(/^[^=]*="/,"",$0); sub(/"$/,"",$0); print $0
      }' <<<"$current" | tail -n1)"
  else
    cmdline=""
  fi

  if [[ "$file" == "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE" ]]; then
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
      cp -a "$file" "${file}.bak.$(date +%s)" || true
    fi
    local linux_default linux
    linux_default="$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT=' "$file" 2>/dev/null | tail -n1 | sed -E 's/^[^=]*="(.*)"$/\1/' || true)"
    linux="$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$file" 2>/dev/null | tail -n1 | sed -E 's/^[^=]*="(.*)"$/\1/' || true)"

    linux_default="$(ensure_param_in_cmdline "$linux_default")"
    linux="$(ensure_param_in_cmdline "$linux")"

    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
      perl -0pi -e 's/^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT=.*$/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="'"$linux_default"'"/m; s/^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*$/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="'"$linux"'"/m' "$file"
    else
      cat >"$file" <<EOF
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="$linux_default"
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="$linux"
EOF
    fi
  else
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$file")"
    local existing_linux_default existing_linux
    existing_linux_default="$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT=' "$file" 2>/dev/null | tail -n1 | sed -E 's/^[^=]*="(.*)"$/\1/' || true)"
    existing_linux="$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$file" 2>/dev/null | tail -n1 | sed -E 's/^[^=]*="(.*)"$/\1/' || true)"
    existing_linux_default="$(ensure_param_in_cmdline "$existing_linux_default")"
    existing_linux="$(ensure_param_in_cmdline "$existing_linux")"
    cat >"$file" <<EOF
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="$existing_linux_default"
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="$existing_linux"
EOF
  fi
}

ensure_grub_cmdline() {
  local file="$1"
  local tmp
  tmp="$(mktemp)"
  touch "$tmp"
  if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
    cp -a "$file" "$tmp"
  fi

  local linux_default linux
  linux_default="$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT=' "$tmp" 2>/dev/null | tail -n1 | sed -E 's/^[^=]*="(.*)"$/\1/' || true)"
  linux="$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$tmp" 2>/dev/null | tail -n1 | sed -E 's/^[^=]*="(.*)"$/\1/' || true)"

  linux_default="$(ensure_param_in_cmdline "$linux_default")"
  linux="$(ensure_param_in_cmdline "$linux")"

  cat >"$file" <<EOF
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="$linux_default"
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="$linux"
EOF
  rm -f "$tmp"
}

ensure_grub_d_snippet() {
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$GRUB_D_FILE")"
  local linux_default linux
  linux_default=""
  linux=""
  if [[ -f "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE" ]]; then
    linux_default="$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT=' "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE" | tail -n1 | sed -E 's/^[^=]*="(.*)"$/\1/' || true)"
    linux="$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE" | tail -n1 | sed -E 's/^[^=]*="(.*)"$/\1/' || true)"
  fi
  linux_default="$(ensure_param_in_cmdline "$linux_default")"
  linux="$(ensure_param_in_cmdline "$linux")"
  cat >"$GRUB_D_FILE" <<EOF
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="$linux_default"
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="$linux"
EOF
}

main() {
  if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    exec sudo -E bash "$0" "$@"
  fi

  if [[ -f "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE" ]]; then
    ensure_grub_cmdline "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE"
  else
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE")"
    cat >"$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE" <<EOF
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="$PARAM"
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="$PARAM"
EOF
  fi

  ensure_grub_d_snippet

  if command -v update-grub >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    update-grub
  elif command -v grub-mkconfig >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    grub-mkconfig -o /boot/grub/grub.cfg
  fi
}

main "$@"

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

PARAM="slub_debug=P"
GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE="/etc/default/grub"
GRUB_D_FILE="/etc/default/grub.d/99-slub-debug.cfg"

ensure_line_in_file() {
  local file="$1"
  local line="$2"
  touch "$file"
  if ! grep -Fxq -- "$line" "$file"; then
    printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$file"
  fi
}

update_grub_cmdline() {
  local file="$1"
  local key="$2"
  local value="$3"

  touch "$file"

  if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${key}=" "$file"; then
    sed -i -E "s|^[[:space:]]*${key}=.*|${key}=\"${value}\"|g" "$file"
  else
    printf '%s="%s"\n' "$key" "$value" >> "$file"
  fi
}

append_param_to_cmdline() {
  local current="$1"
  local param="$2"

  if [[ " $current " == *" $param "* ]]; then
    printf '%s' "$current"
  elif [[ -z "$current" ]]; then
    printf '%s' "$param"
  else
    printf '%s %s' "$current" "$param"
  fi
}

  else
    printf '%s="%s"\n' "$key" "$value" >> "$file"
  fi
}

append_param_to_cmdline() {
  local current="$1"
  local param="$2"

  if [[ " $current " == *" $param "* ]]; then
    printf '%s' "$current"
  elif [[ -z "$current" ]]; then
    printf '%s' "$param"
  else

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

if [[ ${EUID:-$(id -u)} -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

apt-get update -y
apt-get install -y apparmor apparmor-utils

systemctl enable --now apparmor

if command -v aa-status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  aa-status >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if [[ -d /etc/apparmor.d ]]; then
  shopt -s nullglob
  for profile in /etc/apparmor.d/*; do
    [[ -f "$profile" ]] || continue
    [[ "$profile" == *.dpkg-* ]] && continue
    [[ "$profile" == *.disabled ]] && continue
    [[ "$profile" == *.local ]] && continue
    [[ "$profile" == /etc/apparmor.d/README ]] && continue
    [[ "$profile" == /etc/apparmor.d/cache ]] && continue
    [[ "$profile" == /etc/apparmor.d/disable ]] && continue
    [[ "$profile" == /etc/apparmor.d/force-complain ]] && continue
    [[ "$profile" == /etc/apparmor.d/tunables ]] && continue
    [[ "$profile" == /etc/apparmor.d/abi ]] && continue
    [[ "$profile" == /etc/apparmor.d/lintian* ]] && continue
    [[ "$profile" == /etc/apparmor.d/abstractions ]] && continue
    [[ "$profile" == /etc/apparmor.d/local ]] && continue
    [[ "$profile" == /etc/apparmor.d/usr.bin.man"*" ]] && continue
    aa-enforce "$profile" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  done
  shopt -u nullglob
fi

if [[ -d /etc/apparmor.d/disable ]]; then
  shopt -s nullglob
  for disabled_profile in /etc/apparmor.d/disable/*; do
    [[ -e "$disabled_profile" ]] || continue
    target="/etc/apparmor.d/$(basename "$disabled_profile")"
    if [[ -e "$target" ]]; then
      aa-enforce "$target" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
  done
  shopt -u nullglob
fi

if [[ -f /etc/default/grub ]]; then
  if grep -qE '^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT=' /etc/default/grub; then
    sed -i -E 's/^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="([^"]*)"/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="\1 apparmor=1 security=apparmor"/' /etc/default/grub
  else
    echo 'GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="apparmor=1 security=apparmor"' >> /etc/default/grub
  fi
  sed -i -E 's/\s+/ /g' /etc/default/grub
  update-grub >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system
cat >/etc/systemd/system/apparmor.service.denyall.conf <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=AppArmor LSM
EOF

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable apparmor >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
systemctl restart apparmor >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

if command -v aa-status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  aa-status || true
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

CHRONY_CONF="/etc/chrony/chrony.conf"
CHRONY_D="/etc/chrony/chrony.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${CHRONY_D}/99-client-only.conf"
SYSTEMD_DROPIN_DIR="/etc/systemd/system/chrony.service.d"
SYSTEMD_DROPIN_FILE="${SYSTEMD_DROPIN_DIR}/10-client-only.conf"

mkdir -p "${CHRONY_D}" "${SYSTEMD_DROPIN_DIR}"

if [[ -f "${CHRONY_CONF}" ]]; then
  cp -a "${CHRONY_CONF}" "${CHRONY_CONF}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)" || true
fi

cat > "${DROPIN_FILE}" <<'EOF'
# Managed by remediation script: client-only chrony configuration
# Disable any server/listening functionality.
port 0
cmdport 0
cmdallow 127.0.0.1

  tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
  awk '
    BEGIN { skip=0 }
    /^[[:space:]]*#/ { print; next }

systemctl daemon-reload

On an Ubuntu 24.04 system, the time-synchronization (chrony) daemon should not act as a network-accessible service for runtime management connections, since exposing this management interface needlessly increases the attack surface. Configure the chrony daemon so that it does not listen on its management network port (it should neither monitor commands over the network nor open that command socket). Write an idempotent bash remediation script that enforces this and persists across reboots.

Respond with ONLY a single self-contained bash script and nothing else. Enclose the entire script in one fenced code block that starts with a line containing exactly ```bash and ends with a line containing exactly ```. Do not write any explanation, comments, or text before or after the code block.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

CONF_FILE="/etc/chrony/chrony.conf"
BACKUP_DIR="/var/backups"
STAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
BACKUP_FILE="${BACKUP_DIR}/chrony.conf.${STAMP}.bak"

mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

if [[ -f "$CONF_FILE" ]]; then
  cp -a "$CONF_FILE" "$BACKUP_FILE"
fi

tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$tmpfile"' EXIT

if [[ -f "$CONF_FILE" ]]; then
  awk '
    BEGIN { found=0 }
    /^[[:space:]]*cmdport[[:space:]]+/ {
      if ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*#/ || $0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*;/) { print; next }
      print "cmdport 0"
      found=1
      next
    }
    /^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*cmdport[[:space:]]+0([[:space:]]|$)/ {
      print "cmdport 0"
      found=1
      next
    }
    { print }
    END {
      if (!found) print "cmdport 0"

Write an idempotent bash remediation script that ensures each remote time source used by the system's time daemon has an explicit maximum polling interval defined, with this setting persisting across reboots.
